jokes.text: When you look in the mirror and you see your push-up bra and your fake eyelashes and your makeup and your press-on nails, the principles that I'm applying to the office are the same ones that have made Lady Gaga a star. Or any number of drag queens.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining his office makeover as 'cosmetic tweaks'
jokes.punchline: Comparing office improvements to drag queen aesthetics
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael Scott absurdist logic - connecting office decor to Lady Gaga with complete sincerity. Strong character integration and memorable comparison.

jokes.text: I am Computron, your answer to everything.
jokes.punchline: Dwight in ridiculous costume pretending to be a computer
jokes.explanation: Dwight's commitment to absurd schemes is perfectly in character. Visual comedy relies on costume/performance.

jokes.text: What is the world's largest ocean? Calculating. Pacific!
jokes.punchline: Dwight obviously just answering basic trivia question
jokes.explanation: Simple gag about fake technology. Gets mild laughs but nothing special.

jokes.text: Great news, Michael, we're now the official paper supplier of the NFL. That is fantastic. It's good, but it's not good enough. Keep working.
jokes.punchline: Michael dismissing obviously fake NFL contract news
jokes.explanation: Michael's casual dismissal of impossible good news reveals his delusion. Character-driven absurdity.

jokes.text: She's our international sales consultant.
jokes.setup: Michael introducing Pam to important banker
jokes.punchline: Inflating receptionist role to sound impressive
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael overselling - perfectly economical character moment but predictable.

jokes.text: It might seem crazy, but since there's no one left in New York, Michael is Dunder Mifflin's highest ranking employee.
jokes.punchline: Michael's authority is pure accident of circumstance
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan delivery with economy. Explains absurd situation matter-of-factly.

jokes.text: For the record, not onboard with fake Stanley.
jokes.setup: Camera shows Stanley at his desk
jokes.punchline: Stanley breaking fourth wall about office improvements
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stanley moment - his disdain for Michael's schemes while acknowledging understanding. Great meta humor.

jokes.text: They make the best Maine lobster in the world. You'll love it. Mul Yam in Tel Aviv is better. No, Computron, actually, I think Coopers is the best.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to impress banker with restaurant choice
jokes.punchline: Arguing with 'Computron' about lobster restaurants
jokes.explanation: Michael's commitment to the bit even when it backfires. Good character integration.

jokes.text: Actually, I am a fact checker.
jokes.setup: Banker explaining he's a 'glorified fact checker'
jokes.punchline: Deflating his own description even further
jokes.explanation: Nice deflation moment with good economy but minor character.

jokes.text: I left a copy of Best American Mystery Stories 1999 in Toby's favorite stall. So, yes, I think I bought us some time.
jokes.setup: Jim talking head about delaying tactics
jokes.punchline: Revealing intimate knowledge of Toby's bathroom reading habits
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jim prank - specific, character-driven, and unexpectedly intimate knowledge. High craft.

jokes.text: He has more character in a single flake of his dandruff than you have in that entire snow bank on your shoulder.
jokes.setup: Banker calling Michael 'a bit of a character'
jokes.punchline: Dwight's overly elaborate defense with dandruff/snow metaphor
jokes.explanation: Peak Dwight loyalty with creative insult structure. Character integration is perfect.

jokes.text: Ignore him. He's the local lunatic. Come on, Dwight, get out of here. Dwight? Who is this Dwight? Oh! You mean Dwight Schrute, the company's top salesman and the creator of Computron.
jokes.setup: Jim trying to dismiss Dwight as lunatic
jokes.punchline: Dwight immediately proving it by refusing to break character
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim-Dwight dynamic. Dwight's commitment to absurd bit makes Jim's insult backfire.

jokes.text: I wear many hats, but the one I'm wearing currently is that of gracious host.
jokes.punchline: Talking about 'wearing hats' while literally wearing ridiculous headgear
jokes.explanation: Visual pun works with Dwight's formal speech pattern but not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: Um... Nothing comes to mind.
jokes.setup: Asked about safety issues and potential lawsuits
jokes.punchline: Toby's obvious lie given what we know about this office
jokes.explanation: Perfect Toby moment - his helpless incompetence in HR. Economy and character integration strong.

jokes.text: Mike, get off of the lift. Please! Come on, now. I'm fine. I'm fine. I'm fine.
jokes.setup: Cut to warehouse scene with Michael on forklift
jokes.punchline: Michael immediately proving safety isn't under control
jokes.explanation: Good comedic timing cutting from Toby's lie to immediate contradiction but fairly standard physical comedy.

jokes.text: Today, smoking is going to save lives.
jokes.punchline: Dwight's backwards logic about smoking in fire emergency
jokes.explanation: Brilliant paradox delivered with Dwight's absolute certainty. Highly quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: Stay alive, I'm getting help! Pull me up! You're too heavy. I only weigh 82 pounds. Save Bandit!
jokes.setup: Fire drill chaos with people in ceiling
jokes.punchline: Angela's ridiculous weight claim and cat priority
jokes.explanation: Classic fire drill chaos. Angela's vanity and cat obsession perfectly integrated.

jokes.text: Yeah. It's the safest part of a car. In the event of a crash, the driver always protects his side first.
jokes.setup: Asked why he sits in back seat
jokes.punchline: Dwight's paranoid survival logic about car crashes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight paranoia with twisted logic that almost makes sense. Strong character integration.

jokes.text: My hair! Get off! Get off me! I got it! I got it! Get it off! You're welcome.
jokes.punchline: Stanley's rare moment of panic and exertion
jokes.explanation: Funny to see normally calm Stanley in chaos but fairly standard physical comedy.

jokes.text: Happy is a funny word. In what way? You know... You know, what does it mean to be happy? Keep philosophers busy for a while.
jokes.punchline: Toby's overthinking deflection into philosophy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Toby - his inability to give straight answers and tendency to overcomplicate simple questions.

jokes.text: It would be better if you were unconscious. Try my googy googy.
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate suggestions and made-up medical terms
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael incompetence with made-up expertise. Moderately cringey.

jokes.text: Can't we all just get along? Or have we forgotten the words of the Reverend King?
jokes.punchline: Michael's inappropriate MLK reference for minor workplace dispute
jokes.explanation: Michael's inappropriate historical references are in character but this feels like well-worn territory.

jokes.text: You don't know? I don't know. You're the head of human resources. I don't know.
jokes.punchline: Toby's complete helplessness escalating with repetition
jokes.explanation: Toby's incompetence escalating through repetition. Good structure but predictable for character.

jokes.text: Stanley? How about that hot picture you have by your desk? Centerfold in the Catholic schoolgirl's outfit? I mean, it is hot, it is sexy, and it turns him on. And I will admit, best part of my morning is staring at it. But what? Are we gonna just take it away? That is my daughter, she goes to Catholic girls' school.
jokes.punchline: Revelation it's Stanley's daughter in school uniform
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - builds up inappropriate scenario then deflates with innocent explanation. High impact.

jokes.text: You said, come in! No, I didn't! Just, please, get out. Oh, my God! What's wrong with my outfit? You might consider pulling it down a touch. Bunch of prudes.
jokes.setup: Michael accidentally walking in on Meredith
jokes.punchline: Meredith's complete lack of shame about inappropriate dress
jokes.explanation: Classic Meredith shamelessness but fairly standard for the character. Visual comedy implied.

jokes.text: Meredith, your boob is out. Fine.
jokes.punchline: Creed's matter-of-fact observation and Meredith's casual response
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character voices. Creed's bluntness and Meredith's shamelessness.

jokes.text: Damn it, Meredith, where are your panties? It's casual day.
jokes.punchline: Her logic that casual day explains lack of underwear
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Meredith's twisted logic about dress codes.

jokes.text: You know what? The only thing I am worried about is getting a boner.
jokes.setup: Michael claiming he's not worried about HR complaints
jokes.punchline: Admitting his only concern is inappropriate arousal
jokes.explanation: Michael's inappropriate oversharing is in character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Number one, inverted penis. Could you mean vagina? Because if you do, I want that covered.
jokes.punchline: Meredith's literal interpretation and demand for coverage
jokes.explanation: Meredith's shameless practicality about intimate medical issues. Decent cringe comedy.

jokes.text: I thought your vagina was removed during your hysterectomy. A uterus is different from a vagina. I still have a vagina.
jokes.punchline: Meredith having to explain female anatomy
jokes.explanation: Kevin's ignorance and Meredith's matter-of-fact correction fit characters but fairly standard.

jokes.text: You can consider this my retirement from comedy. Does that include 'that's what she said'? Yes. Wow, that is really hard. You really think you can go all day long? Well, you always left me satisfied and smiling, so... That's what she said!
jokes.setup: Michael vowing to retire his catchphrase
jokes.punchline: Immediately breaking vow with perfect setup
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup and payoff structure. Michael's inability to resist perfect setup is character-driven and well-executed.

jokes.text: Why did you get it so big? That's what she said. Does the skin look red and swollen? That's what she said. That's my joke. Damn it, Dwight.
jokes.punchline: Dwight stealing the catchphrase repeatedly
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Dwight appropriating Michael's thing. Michael's frustration is perfect.

jokes.text: Eat it, Phyllis. Dip it in the water so it'll slide down your gullet more easily. That's what she said.
jokes.punchline: Michael can't resist inappropriate interpretation
jokes.explanation: Continuing the running gag but this example is more forced and less clever.

jokes.text: I don't know how I'm going to get through this.
jokes.setup: Michael overwhelmed by avoiding his catchphrase
jokes.punchline: His desperation over giving up simple joke
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's dependence on his humor crutch.

jokes.text: I don't want to lie. And I don't want to tell the truth.
jokes.punchline: His paralyzed middle ground of HR incompetence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Toby summation - his complete ineffectiveness crystallized in two sentences. High craft and economy.

jokes.text: H.R. Pufnstuf. Right up my alley.
jokes.punchline: Michael's childish wordplay reference
jokes.explanation: Michael's childish humor is in character but this reference feels dated and low-effort.

jokes.text: No. This company does not waste time or resources ever.
jokes.punchline: Michael's emphatic denial given all the evidence
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael delusion - denying obvious reality with complete conviction.

jokes.text: Flernenton. Flonkerton.
jokes.punchline: Jim's made-up sport name correction
jokes.explanation: Jim's deadpan commitment to absurd made-up Olympics terminology. Great economy and character.

jokes.text: Happy holidays, Dwight. But do not open it till Christmas. You're so pathetic. Well, I hope it was worth it, because I'm going to take it apart in about five minutes. I think it'll take you a little bit longer than that.
jokes.setup: Jim giving Dwight wrapped desk gift
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's Dwight's own desk wrapped up
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim prank with perfect setup and reveal. High originality and character integration.

jokes.text: If I can skin a mule deer in less than 10 minutes, I ought to be able to cut my... Damn it! Jim!
jokes.punchline: His skill comparison makes the defeat more humiliating
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight character moment - bizarre skill comparison makes his frustration funnier.

jokes.text: He put my stuff in Jell-O again. Bang, boom, shake, klump!
jokes.punchline: Michael's nonsensical sound effects response
jokes.explanation: References classic prank but Michael's response is random rather than clever.

jokes.text: Question, what kind of bear is best? That's a ridiculous question. False. Black bear. Well, that's debatable. There are basically two schools of thought. Fact, bears eat beets. Bears, beets, Battlestar Galactica.
jokes.setup: Jim impersonating Dwight with bear question
jokes.punchline: Perfect escalation to absurd three B's connection
jokes.explanation: Iconic Office moment - perfect Dwight impersonation with brilliant escalating logic. Highly quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: Identity theft is not a joke, Jim! Millions of families suffer every year!
jokes.punchline: Dwight's over-serious PSA response to costume
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight overreaction - treating costume prank as serious crime. Highly quotable and character-perfect.

jokes.text: This is Creed. And he is in charge of something. Right? That is correct.
jokes.punchline: Vague job description and Creed's confident agreement
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Creed's mysterious role and Michael's vague management style.

jokes.text: I know exactly what he's talking about. I sprout mung beans on a damp paper towel in my desk drawer. Very nutritious, but they smell like death.
jokes.setup: Question about Creed having an old man smell
jokes.punchline: Creed's matter-of-fact explanation of desk farming
jokes.explanation: Peak Creed weirdness - specific, bizarre, delivered matter-of-factly. High originality and character integration.

jokes.text: Hey, where's Dwight? You didn't hear? Decapitated. Whole big thing. We had a funeral for a bird.
jokes.punchline: Creed's casual announcement of impossible death and bird funeral
jokes.explanation: Another iconic Creed moment - escalating absurdity delivered deadpan. Perfect economy and character voice.

jokes.text: I'm pretty sure none of that's real. You're not real, man.
jokes.setup: Jim's logical response to Creed's story
jokes.punchline: Creed's philosophical reversal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed follow-up - turning skepticism into existential question. Great economy.

jokes.text: This is a building where friends become lovers, and lovers become sexually interactive, right?
jokes.punchline: His awkward escalation of relationship terminology
jokes.explanation: Michael's inappropriate workplace observations with awkward phrasing. Moderately cringey.

jokes.text: I like you. But you need to access your un-crazy side.
jokes.punchline: His blunt assessment with made-up psychology term
jokes.explanation: Perfect Darryl - direct, made-up terminology delivered seriously. Good character integration.

jokes.text: Darryl Philbin is the most complicated man that I've ever met. I mean, who says exactly what they're thinking? What kind of game is that?
jokes.punchline: Finding honesty confusing and suspicious
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kelly logic - finding straightforward communication baffling. Strong character integration.

jokes.text: Snip, snap! Snip, snap! Snip, snap! I did! You have no idea the physical toll that three vasectomies have on a person!
jokes.punchline: The shocking revelation of three vasectomies with sound effects
jokes.explanation: Legendary Office moment - perfect escalation, shocking revelation, iconic delivery. This is the gold standard for TV comedy.

jokes.text: Well, I hope you're still committed, because I'm pregnant.
jokes.setup: Ryan saying he was committed to their relationship
jokes.punchline: Kelly's bombshell fake pregnancy announcement
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and character move for Kelly - using pregnancy to manipulate. Strong structure.

jokes.text: Jim has got it bad for Pam. Oh! Which one is Pam?
jokes.punchline: Banker's complete ignorance after spending day in office
jokes.explanation: Shows banker's detachment and Michael's assumption about obvious office dynamics.

jokes.text: But I bet Jim got the job. I mean, why wouldn't he? He's totally qualified and smart. Everyone loves him. If he never comes back again, that's okay. We're friends. We just... We never got the timing right. But you know what, it's okay. I'm totally fine. Everything is going to be totally... Pam. Sorry. Are you free for dinner tonight? Yes. All right. Then it's a date.
jokes.setup: Pam's talking head about Jim leaving and accepting his absence
jokes.punchline: Jim interrupting at perfect moment to ask her out
jokes.explanation: Perfect romantic comedy moment - Pam's resignation interrupted by Jim's return. High emotional impact.

jokes.text: Pam, will you marry me? Oh, my God!
jokes.setup: Romantic moment building from previous scene
jokes.punchline: Jim's proposal coming out of nowhere
jokes.explanation: Major character moment with perfect escalation from date to proposal. High emotional payoff.

jokes.text: What is Jim capable of? Or Pam? Or Kevin?
jokes.setup: Michael's philosophical reflection on staff potential
jokes.punchline: Including Kevin in list of people with unlimited potential
jokes.explanation: Michael's sincere belief in everyone's potential, including obviously limited Kevin. Sweet character moment.

jokes.text: Computron experiencing emotion. Computron, I'm going to pull your plug, okay, buddy? Please don't. Computron wants to live. Shut... Shut up. Shut up.
jokes.punchline: Dwight's commitment to character even when being 'killed'
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to Computron bit - Dwight's method acting commitment and Michael's frustration.

jokes.text: Sittin' in my office With a plate of grilled bacon Call my man Dwight Just to see what was shakin'! Yo, Mike, our town is dope and pretty So check out how we live In the Electric City! They call it Scranton! What? The Electric City!
jokes.setup: Michael and Dwight's rap about Scranton
jokes.punchline: The earnest celebration of mundane city features
jokes.explanation: Iconic Scranton rap - absurd civic pride delivered with complete sincerity. Became cultural phenomenon.
